SGM (Small Graph Model) is the trained model, a Graph-to-Graph function; SIM is the simulator and sole source of truth; LLM (Qwen) is a translator layer supplying language surfaces only; the Architect (a frontier model) authors low-volume high-leverage artifacts.

The SGM processes language inside graph nodes, structure across edges, and reasons over multiple graphs and non-graph data to produce graph output; it is never the world model, only the interface between language and the SIM state and actions. v0 realizes it as the existing decoder over a canonical, invertible graph serialization; graph-native input and output is the successor architecture. The LLM never supplies facts or logic. The Architect authors doctrine, schemas, surface-bank specs, and hard evaluation cases as versioned repo files reviewed like code, and never writes corpus tokens directly.

The four-role division makes the earlier informal division of labor mechanical. The SIM runs forward cheaply and adjudicates any state on demand, so ground truth is computed, never stored. The LLM contributes language structures into offline surface banks and translates at the human boundary, and is evicted from the inner training loop entirely. The Architect role formalizes direct involvement of a capable model at crucial points: one carefully authored decision doctrine or scenario family is instantiated thousands of times by the SIM, so high-level judgment enters the corpus as structure rather than sampled behavior, which is why this is not distillation. The SGM name is deliberately close to SLM: the model class is the same decoder for v0, and what changes is the contract, from text-to-text assistant to Graph-to-Graph controller function whose encoding happens to be a token stream.
